channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Annual CUSMA Reviews Create Uncertainty for North American Businesses
item.description: The article discusses the impact of U.S. President Donald Trump's trade policies on the Canada-U.S. Free Trade Agreement (CUSMA), emphasizing how his tactics of imposing annual reviews create uncertai...

item.title: Understanding Legal Objections in Trials and Their Importance in Due Process
item.description: The article explains the legal mechanisms of objections during trials, emphasizing their role in safeguarding due process. It highlights how lawyers from both prosecution and defense interrupt each ot...

item.title: Evening News Round-up: Trump Declares Iran Nuclear Deal Effectively Over, Ramesh Mhatre Arrested, NCERT Revises Class 8 Textbook and Other Top Developments
item.description: This evening news round-up brings together some of the day's most significant developments from India and around the world. US President Donald Trump stated that the interim nuclear agreement with Ira...

item.title: Albury-Wodonga Weather Forecast for Today
item.description: The article explains that News Corp Australia has implemented software to manage crawler bot traffic on its websites. Readers or subscribers who encounter a block message are advised to temporarily di...

item.title: Kevin Bacon's Roller Disco Beginnings in 1979
item.description: This article recounts Kevin Bacon's early career as a roller disco model in 1979, long before he became a Hollywood icon. As a struggling actor working as a busboy in Manhattan, Bacon embraced the rol...

item.title: Cape Town couple faces fraud charges over Zakah fund misappropriation
item.description: A Cape Town couple is set to appear in court after allegedly defrauding a non-profit organisation of R171,350 intended for Islamic studies with underprivileged youth. The suspects, aged 41 and 48, wer...

item.title: Parliament Presses for Recovery of KSh251 Million Owed to Kenya Literature Bureau by State Agencies
item.description: The Kenya Literature Bureau (KLB) has appealed to Parliament for support in recovering more than KSh251.5 million owed by government institutions, saying current government rules prevent one state age...

item.title: Trump Ends Iran Ceasefire, Threatens Trade Cut with Spain Amid NATO Tensions
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump disrupted a NATO summit by demanding an end to trade with Spain and reviving claims over Greenland, which strained relations with NATO allies. During a meeting in Ankara wi...

item.title: Zanzibar's Political Reconciliation Efforts to Strengthen National Unity
item.description: The article discusses Zanzibar's political reconciliation process aimed at bridging divides between the two main parties, Chama cha Mapinduzi (CCM) and ACT Wazalendo. Dr. Dennis Muchunguzi, an expert ...

item.title: Neo-Nazi convicted of terrorism after MI5 sting operation
item.description: Alfie Coleman, a 22-year-old supermarket worker from Essex, has been sentenced to 13.5 years in prison for planning a terrorist attack. The neo-Nazi, described as a 'militant accelerationist', was lur...

item.title: Recent Balochistan Attacks Result in 38 Martyrs and 54 Terrorists Neutralized
item.description: The DG ISPR provided detailed accounts of three major terrorist incidents in Balochistan over the past four days, highlighting the ongoing security challenges. The first incident occurred on July 4/5 ...

item.title: Egypt Call for Referee Investigation After World Cup Exit to Argentina
item.description: The Egyptian Football Association (EFA) has officially complained to FIFA, asking for the officials who officiated Egypt's 3-2 loss to Argentina in the World Cup last 16 to be removed. The match, held...

item.title: Hamilton Considers Moratorium on Data Centres Amid Regulatory and Environmental Concerns
item.description: Hamilton city council has proposed a moratorium on data centre developments, potentially making it the first Canadian city to implement such a pause. Researcher Anne Pasek, a Canada Research Chair in ...

item.title: Cyberabad launches week-long traffic survey to address IT corridor congestion
item.description: Cyberabad traffic police are planning a comprehensive week-long vehicle survey to address growing traffic congestion in the IT corridor. The initiative, approved during a review meeting chaired by Joi...

item.title: Netflix and e.tv Partner for African Content Distribution
item.description: Netflix and e.tv have entered into a groundbreaking content partnership, marking a significant milestone in African media. The agreement, which begins with the simulcast of the telenovela 'The Four of...

item.title: Families Of Missing NELAN Engineers Demand Justice Over Judicial Irregularities In Ebonyi Case
item.description: The families of five missing NELAN Consulting Ltd engineers have petitioned the National Judicial Council (NJC), Nigerian Bar Association, and other bodies over alleged judicial irregularities in the ...

item.title: Denmark Stands Firm on Greenland as Trump's NATO Comments Resurface
item.description: President Donald Trump has reignited tensions with NATO allies by suggesting the United States should control Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. During a NATO summit in Turkey, Trump reite...

item.title: Blood Test Research Aims to Detect Cancer Recurrence
item.description: Researchers at Princess Margaret Cancer Centre in Toronto are conducting a large-scale trial called SHERLOCK to determine if a blood test can detect microscopic traces of cancer remaining after treatm...

item.title: Economic Challenges Despite Upper-Middle Income Status in the Philippines
item.description: The Philippines has been classified as an upper-middle-income country, yet many Filipinos still face economic hardships. The article explores the reasons behind this discrepancy, highlighting factors ...

item.title: Pakistan Navy locates wreckage of missing K2 Airways cargo plane
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) confirmed that the wreckage of a missing K2 Airways cargo Boeing 737 aircraft has been located 53 nautical miles south of Ormara after a 12-hour search operation ...
